all.en: For eleven years, Hannah had taught middle-school history in a quiet suburb of Taichung.
all.zh: 整整十一年，Hannah 都在台中一個安靜的郊區教國中歷史。

all.en: She loved her students, but the long grading nights had slowly drained her energy.
all.zh: 她愛她的學生，但漫長的批改作業夜晚慢慢耗盡了她的精力。

all.en: One winter afternoon she watched her younger brother solve a coding puzzle on his laptop.
all.zh: 某個冬日下午，她看著弟弟在筆電上解一道程式設計題。

all.en: He explained loops and functions with such excitement that something inside her shifted.
all.zh: 他興奮地解釋著迴圈與函式，她內心似乎有什麼東西被觸動了。

all.en: That night she searched for online bootcamps designed for adults switching careers.
all.zh: 那晚她搜尋了為轉換職涯的成年人所設計的線上密集訓練課程。

all.en: She bookmarked three programs and compared their prices, schedules, and graduate reviews carefully.
all.zh: 她把三個課程加入書籤，並仔細比較它們的費用、時程與畢業生評價。

all.en: Telling her principal was the hardest part of the decision.
all.zh: 告訴校長是整個決定中最困難的部分。

all.en: He looked sad but wished her well and asked her to stay until the term ended.
all.zh: 他看起來很難過，但仍祝她一切順利，並請她做到學期結束。

all.en: Her parents worried about the steady salary she was about to give up.
all.zh: 她的父母擔心她即將放棄的那份穩定薪水。

all.en: Hannah assured them she had saved enough to live simply for at least a year.
all.zh: Hannah 向他們保證她存的錢足以樸素地生活至少一年。

all.en: The bootcamp started on a chilly March morning with twenty other career changers.
all.zh: 密集訓練課程在三月一個寒冷的早晨開始，同班還有另外二十位轉職者。

all.en: Hannah felt out of place among nurses, accountants, and a former chef named Leo.
all.zh: Hannah 在護士、會計師與一位名叫 Leo 的前主廚之中感到格格不入。

all.en: Their instructor, a patient woman named Iris, told them that confusion was part of learning.
all.zh: 他們的講師是一位耐心的女士，名叫 Iris，她告訴他們困惑是學習的一部分。

all.en: The first week introduced variables, conditions, and the cheerful rituals of debugging.
all.zh: 第一週介紹了變數、條件，以及除錯時那些苦中作樂的日常儀式。

all.en: Hannah stayed up past midnight, rereading lessons and rewriting tiny example programs.
all.zh: Hannah 熬夜到過了午夜，重讀課程內容並重新撰寫小小的範例程式。

all.en: By week three, she could build a simple to-do list app that actually worked.
all.zh: 到了第三週，她已經能做出一個真正能運作的簡單待辦事項應用程式。

all.en: Her old teacher's instinct returned when classmates began to ask her for help.
all.zh: 當同學們開始向她求助時，她身為老師的本能也回來了。

all.en: She explained difficult ideas using stories, just as she had done with history lessons.
all.zh: 她用故事說明困難的概念，就像她以前上歷史課時那樣。

all.en: Iris noticed and quietly suggested she consider a teaching role inside the tech industry.
all.zh: Iris 注意到了，便悄悄建議她考慮在科技業從事教學相關的工作。

all.en: Halfway through the program, Hannah hit a wall during a sorting algorithms unit.
all.zh: 課程進行到一半時，Hannah 在排序演算法的單元上撞了牆。

all.en: She cried for an hour, doubting whether her brain could really learn this.
all.zh: 她哭了一個小時，懷疑自己的腦袋是否真的能學會這些東西。

all.en: Leo, the former chef, called and reminded her that bread also rises slowly in the cold.
all.zh: 前主廚 Leo 打電話給她，提醒她麵包在寒冷中也是慢慢膨脹的。

all.en: His simple metaphor made her laugh and she returned to the lesson the next morning.
all.zh: 他樸實的比喻讓她笑了出來，第二天早晨她又回到課程上。

all.en: The final project required each student to build a real web application from scratch.
all.zh: 結業專題要求每位學生從零開始打造一個真正的網頁應用程式。

all.en: Hannah chose to create a study tool that helped students memorize historical timelines.
all.zh: Hannah 選擇打造一個幫助學生記憶歷史年表的學習工具。

all.en: She combined flashcards, quizzes, and a friendly progress chart on a clean interface.
all.zh: 她在一個簡潔的介面上結合了字卡、測驗與一個友善的進度圖表。

all.en: On demo day, three of her former colleagues attended through a video link.
all.zh: 成果發表日當天，她以前的三位同事透過視訊連線出席。

all.en: They smiled with pride as Hannah presented her project with steady confidence.
all.zh: 當 Hannah 帶著沉穩的自信介紹她的專題時，他們驕傲地微笑著。

all.en: An education-technology company in the audience asked her to interview the following week.
all.zh: 現場一家教育科技公司請她在下週前去面試。

all.en: The interview felt almost like a conversation between people who shared the same mission.
all.zh: 那場面試感覺起來幾乎像是擁有共同使命的人之間的一場對話。

all.en: Three days later they offered her a junior developer position on their learning platform.
all.zh: 三天後，他們提供她他們學習平台上的初階開發工程師職位。

all.en: The salary was lower than she had hoped, but the team and product felt right.
all.zh: 薪水比她期望的低了一些，但這個團隊與這項產品感覺是對的。

all.en: On her first day, Hannah introduced herself by saying she had once taught real classrooms.
all.zh: 上班第一天，Hannah 自我介紹時提到她曾經教過真正的教室。

all.en: Her new manager smiled and replied that good teachers always make excellent engineers.
all.zh: 她的新主管笑了笑回答說，好老師永遠能成為優秀的工程師。

all.en: Hannah walked out that evening feeling that her two careers had finally found each other.
all.zh: Hannah 那天傍晚走出公司，感覺她的兩個職涯終於彼此找到了對方。

all.en: Her first task was to redesign the onboarding flow for new high-school users.
all.zh: 她的第一項任務是為新加入的高中使用者重新設計新手導覽流程。

all.en: She drew on her classroom memories to anticipate where students might feel lost or frustrated.
all.zh: 她借助課堂上的回憶，預想學生可能會在哪些地方感到迷惘或挫折。

all.en: Within a month her flow lifted the completion rate by nearly fifteen percent.
all.zh: 不到一個月，她設計的流程把完成率提升了將近百分之十五。

all.en: Her teammates began inviting her to brainstorm sessions for the upcoming science modules.
all.zh: 團隊夥伴開始邀請她參加即將推出的自然科模組的腦力激盪會議。

all.en: Hannah suggested adding short stories about real scientists between the dry technical lessons.
all.zh: Hannah 建議在乾燥的技術課程之間加入關於真實科學家的小故事。

all.en: The product manager loved the idea and asked her to write the first three pilots.
all.zh: 產品經理很喜歡這個想法，便請她撰寫前三個試行的故事。

all.en: She wrote until late at night, surprised by how much she enjoyed weaving narrative with code.
all.zh: 她寫到深夜，訝異於自己有多麼喜歡將敘事與程式碼交織在一起。

all.en: Iris messaged from the bootcamp to ask if Hannah might mentor next semester's students.
all.zh: Iris 從密集訓練課程那邊傳訊息來，詢問 Hannah 是否願意擔任下學期學生的導師。

all.en: Hannah agreed at once, remembering how badly she had needed encouragement during her own start.
all.zh: Hannah 立刻答應了，想起自己剛起步時是多麼需要鼓勵。

all.en: She closed her laptop that night feeling that teaching, in every form, was truly her calling.
all.zh: 那晚她闔上筆電，感覺到無論以哪種形式，教學都真正是她的志業。
